interface.name: openDebugger
interface.brief: start node-inspector and open a debugger window in your browser

interface.name: openConsole
interface.brief: open a pdg console window

interface.name: openCommandPort
interface.brief: start a REPL server on a TCP port

interface.name: createSerializableObject
interface.brief: Creates a pdg.ISerializable object from a JavaScript object with serialization methods

interface.name: FileManager
interface.note: Primary access via singleton instance: pdg.fs

interface.name: LogManager
interface.note: Primary access via singleton instance: pdg.lm

interface.name: ConfigManager
interface.note: Primary access via singleton instance: pdg.cfg

interface.name: ResourceManager
interface.note: Primary access via singleton instance: pdg.res

interface.name: serialize_obj
interface.brief: Serializes an ISerializable object

interface.name: sizeof_obj
interface.brief: Calculates the serialized size of an ISerializable object

interface.name: addHandler
interface.brief: add a new handler for some event type, or for all events if no type specified.  \param inHandler the object to handle events \param inEventType the type of event to handle

interface.name: removeHandler
interface.brief:
remove a handler for some event type, or for all events (see note) if no type specified. If the handler is listed multiple times it will only remove it once.
NOTE: inType == all_events doesn't work quite like you might expect. If you have registered a handler for multiple events, but not with all_events, doing removeHandler(handler, all_events) will do nothing. Basically, all_events is a special event type that matches all event types when considering whether to invoke a handler or not.
It is safe to call remove handler from within an event handler's handleEvent() call. \param inHandler the object to handle events \param inEventType the type of event to stop handling (see note)

interface.name: clear
interface.brief: remove all handlers

interface.name: blockEvent
interface.brief: temporarily ignore all events of a particular type. Events that are blocked are NOT cached for later, they are just dropped. \param inEventType the type of event to block

interface.name: unblockEvent
interface.brief: stop ignoring events of a particular type  \param inEventType the type of event to unblock

interface.name: onTimeout
interface.brief: setup handler to be called once after delay ms

interface.name: onInterval
interface.brief: setup handler to be called regularly at interval ms

interface.name: flipX
interface.brief: flips the object horizontally, center offset is flipped too. Applied before other transformations.

interface.name: flipY
interface.brief: flips the object vertically, center offset is flipped too. Applied before other transformations.

interface.name: isFlippedX
interface.brief: returns true if the object is flipped horizontally

interface.name: isFlippedY
interface.brief: returns true if the object is flipped vertically

interface.name: isFirstContact
interface.brief: true if this is the first moment of contact between the objects, false if part of an ongoing collison

interface.brief: get opacity of this image: 0.0

interface.note: Factory-only class - use factory function: pdg.createDrawing()

interface.brief: NOT IMPLEMENTED

interface.brief: NOT IMPLEMENTED

interface.name: GraphicsManager
interface.note: Primary access via singleton instance: pdg.gfx

interface.name: getCurrentScreenMode
interface.brief: returns object with width, height, depth and maxWindowRect for specified screen

interface.name: getScreenBounds
interface.brief: returns the bounds (position and size) of a screen in the global coordinate space

interface.name: getNthSupportedScreenMode
interface.brief: returns object with width, height, depth and maxWindowRect for specified screen

interface.name: setScreenMode
interface.brief: changes specified screen to closest matching mode

interface.name: createWindowPort
interface.brief: create windowed drawing port with given dimensions, title and depth

interface.name: createFullScreenPort
interface.brief: create full screen drawing port with given dimensions on given screen, optionally changing depth

interface.name: closeGraphicsPort
interface.brief: close given port, along with its window and restore screen mode if changed

interface.name: closeAllGraphicsPorts
interface.brief: close all active graphics ports (e.g. for test cleanup)

interface.name: createFont
interface.brief: get a font with optional scaling adjust

interface.name: getMainPort
interface.brief: return the primary graphics port

interface.name: switchToFullScreenMode
interface.brief: change a port to fullscreen mode, return true on success

interface.name: switchToWindowMode
interface.brief: change a port to window mode, return true on success

interface.name: inFullScreenMode
interface.brief: return whether primary graphics port is fullscreen or not

interface.name: SoundManager
interface.note: Primary access via singleton instance: pdg.snd

interface.name: Point
interface.brief: create and set x & y values

interface.name: distance
interface.brief: get distance from another point

interface.name: offset
interface.brief: get the offset of this point from another point

interface.name: Offset
interface.brief: create and set x & y values

interface.name: equals
interface.brief: return true if this point is equal to the other

interface.name: notEquals
interface.brief: return true if this point is not equal to the other

interface.name: assign
interface.brief: set this offset equal to the given offset

interface.name: add
interface.brief: add an offset to this one

interface.name: sub
interface.brief: subtract an offset from this one

interface.name: mul
interface.brief: multiply this offset by the given one

interface.name: div
interface.brief: divide this offset by the given one

interface.name: plus
interface.brief: return new offset that is this offset plus given offset

interface.name: minus
interface.brief: return new offset that is this offset minus given offset

interface.name: times
interface.brief: return new offset that is this offset multiplied by given offset

interface.name: dividedby
interface.brief: return new offset that is this offset divided by given offset

interface.name: vector
interface.brief: return a new vector from this offset

interface.name: Vector
interface.brief: create and set x & y values

interface.name: unit
interface.brief: get the unit vector of this vector, ie: A.unit() = (A.x / |A|, A.y / |A|)

interface.name: normal
interface.brief: get the normal of this vector, ie: A.normal() = (-A.y, A.x)

interface.name: dotProduct
interface.brief: get dot product for this vector with a 2nd vector

interface.name: vectorLength
interface.brief: get length as a vector (distance from origin)

interface.name: vectorAngle
interface.brief: get angle (in radians) for this vector

interface.name: projection
interface.brief: projection of a point onto the line defined by this vector, ie: A.projection(B) = ((A . B) / |B|^2) * B

interface.name: Rect
interface.brief: create a new Rect

interface.name: empty
interface.brief: return true if this rectangle is empty (no width or no height)

interface.name: contains
interface.brief: contains([Point] p) return true if the point is inside this rectangle; contains([Rect] r) return true if the rectangle passed in is entirely inside this rectangle

interface.name: overlaps
interface.brief: return true if this rectangle overlaps the other rectangle at all (sharing an edge is not overlapping)

interface.name: leftTop
interface.brief: get top left corner point of this rectangle

interface.name: rightTop
interface.brief: get top right corner point of this rectangle

interface.name: leftBottom
interface.brief: get bottom left corner point of this rectangle

interface.name: rightBottom
interface.brief: get bottom right corner point of this rectangle

interface.name: centerPoint
interface.brief: get center point of this rectangle

interface.name: x1
interface.brief: alias for Rect.left

interface.name: y1
interface.brief: alias for Rect.top

interface.name: x2
interface.brief: alias for Rect.right

interface.name: y2
interface.brief: alias for Rect.bottom

interface.name: width
interface.brief: get the width of this rectangle

interface.name: height
interface.brief: get the height of this rectangle

interface.name: intersection
interface.brief: get a new rectangle that is the overlapping area of the the rectangles

interface.name: unionWith
interface.brief: get the smallest possible new rectangle that contains both rectangles

interface.name: moveLeft
interface.brief: move the rectangle to the left by some amount

interface.name: moveRight
interface.brief: move the rectangle to the right by some amount

interface.name: moveUp
interface.brief: move the rectangle up by some amount

interface.name: moveDown
interface.brief: move the rectangle down by some amount

interface.name: moveXTo
interface.brief: move the rectangle to a particular x location, leaving y unchanged

interface.name: moveYTo
interface.brief: move the rectangle to a particular y location, leaving x unchanged

interface.name: moveTo
interface.brief: moveTo(x,y): the rectangle to a particular (x, y) location; moveTo([Point] p): move the rectangle to a particular point

interface.name: center
interface.brief: center([Point] p): move the rectangle to be centered over a particular point; center([Rect] r): move the rectangle to be centered within/relative to another rectangle

interface.name: setSize
interface.brief: set the size (width & height) of the rectangle

interface.name: horzShrink
interface.brief: reduce the width of the rectangle while leaving the center point unchanged

interface.name: vertShrink
interface.brief: reduce the height of the rectangle while leaving the center point unchanged

interface.name: horzGrow
interface.brief: increase the width of the rectangle while leaving the center point unchanged

interface.name: vertGrow
interface.brief: increase the height of the rectangle while leaving the center point unchanged

interface.name: shrink
interface.brief: reduce the height and width of the rectangle while leaving the center point unchanged

interface.name: grow
interface.brief: increase the height and width of the rectangle while leaving the center point unchanged

interface.name: horzScale
interface.brief: change the x coordinates of the rectangle by a multiplier

interface.name: vertScale
interface.brief: change the y coordinates of the rectangle by a multiplier

interface.name: scale
interface.brief: change the coordinates of the rectangle by a multiplier

interface.name: round
interface.brief: round the coordinates to closest whole number

interface.name: toQuad
interface.brief: convert rectangle to a Quad

interface.name: equals
interface.brief: return true if this rectangle is equal to the given one

interface.name: notEquals
interface.brief: return true if this rectangle is not equal to the given one

interface.name: assign
interface.brief: set this rectangle equal to the given one

interface.name: add
interface.brief: add([Point] p): offset this rectangle's location by adding x & y coordinates of the point; add([Rect] r): add another rectangle to this one by adding corresponding coordinates

interface.name: sub
interface.brief: sub([Point] p): offset this rectangle's location by subtracting x & y coordinates of the point; sub([Rect] r): add another rectangle to this one by subtracting corresponding coordinates

interface.name: mul
interface.brief: mul([Point] p): change this rectangle's location by multiplying by x & y coordinates of the point; mul([Rect] r): change this rect by multiplying by corresponding coordinates of another rectangle

interface.name: div
interface.brief: div([Point] p): change this rectangle's location by dividing by x & y coordinates of the point; div([Rect] r): change this rect by dividing by corresponding coordinates of another rectangle

interface.name: Quad
interface.brief: create a new Quad

interface.name: getBounds
interface.brief: return a rectangle that bounds the quad

interface.name: centerPoint
interface.brief: return the calculated centerpoint of the quad

interface.name: equals
interface.brief: return true if this quad is equal to the given one

interface.name: notEquals
interface.brief: return true if this quad is not equal to the given one

interface.name: contains
interface.brief: returns true if the point is contained within this quad

interface.name: moveLeft
interface.brief: move the quad to the left by some amount

interface.name: moveRight
interface.brief: move the quad to the right by some amount

interface.name: moveUp
interface.brief: move the quad up by some amount

interface.name: moveDown
interface.brief: move the quad down by some amount

interface.name: rotate
interface.brief: rotate(n): rotate the quad by a rotation in radians (around the calculated center point of the quad); rotate(n, [Offset] o): rotate the quad by a rotation in radians around an offset center point

interface.name: RotatedRect
interface.brief: create a new RotatedRect

interface.name: setCenterOffset
interface.brief: set an offset for point around which rotation is applied

interface.name: setRotation
interface.brief: set the rotation of this rectangle to a particular amount in radians

interface.name: rotate
interface.brief: change the rotation of this rectangle by some number of radians

interface.name: getQuad
interface.brief: create a Quad by applying the rectangle's rotation around the center point with offset

interface.name: Color
interface.brief: create and color and set rgb values

interface.name: equals
interface.brief: return true if this color is equal to the other (ignoring alpha)

interface.name: notEquals
interface.brief: return true if this color is not equal to the other (ignoring alpha)

interface.name: assign
interface.brief: set this color equal to the given color

interface.name: convertToGrayscale
interface.brief: convert this color to a matching shade of grey

interface.name: close
interface.brief: close the connection

interface.name: onClose
interface.brief: setup connection close handler

interface.name: onMessage
interface.brief: setup incoming message handler

interface.name: send
interface.brief: send a message via a reliable transport mechanism

interface.name: sendDgram
interface.brief: send a message via the fastest transport mechanism

interface.name: NetClient
interface.brief: create a network client

interface.name: connect
interface.brief: connect to a server for your game

interface.name: onError
interface.brief: setup error handler

interface.name: NetServer
interface.brief: create a network server

interface.name: listen
interface.brief: open the server for incoming connections

interface.name: broadcast
interface.brief: send a message to all connections, with optional filter

interface.name: expectClient
interface.brief: client reservation

interface.name: onError
interface.brief: setup error handler

interface.name: shutdown
interface.brief: close the listener and don't accept new connections
